task: I’m getting ready to take my car in for a routine tire check, and I want to make sure I’m not overlooking anything basic before I hand it over. What should I verify on the paperwork and on the car itself so I can catch any mismatch early?

turns.agent_response:
Good idea. Before you hand it over, do a quick **paperwork + vehicle check** so you can spot any mismatch early.

### On the paperwork
Verify:
- **Your name, phone number, and vehicle info** are correct
- **Make, model, year, color, and license plate/VIN** match your car
- The **service requested** is clearly listed as a tire check / inspection
- Any **existing damage or notes** are documented
- The **odometer reading** is accurate
- The **estimated cost** and any diagnostic or shop fees are clear
- The **authorization section** says what they can do and what requires your approval
- If they’re keeping the car, note the **drop-off time** and expected completion time

### On the car itself
Check and document:
- **All four tires** for tread wear, cracks, bulges, nails, or uneven wear
- **Tire pressure** if you want a baseline before the check
- **Spare tire** condition, if equipped
- **Wheel/rim damage** like curb rash or bends
- **Valve stems and caps**
- **Dashboard warnings** already on before you drop it off
- **Mileage** and fuel level
- Any **existing scratches, dents, or interior issues**
- Take **photos or a quick video** of the car from all sides, plus close-ups of any damage and the tires

### Extra helpful step
When you hand it over, ask them to:
- Confirm the **exact tires/positions** they’ll inspect
- Note whether they’ll **rotate, patch, replace, or just inspect**
- Return **any old parts** if something is replaced
- Call you before doing anything beyond the tire check

If you want, I can give you a **one-minute pre-dropoff checklist** you can use on your phone.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s the right lane. But I’d want a little more specific on the tire side, not just generic car stuff.

What I’d verify before I hand it over:
- **Tire size** on the paperwork matches the sidewall size on the car
- **Load index / speed rating** if they’re talking replacement or ordering anything
- **DOT date code** if they’re claiming a tire is “old” or needs to be swapped
- Whether they’re checking **all four plus the spare**, not just the obvious one
- If there’s an **alignment note** or uneven wear, make sure that gets written down separately
- Any **TPMS light** or sensor issue already present
- Exact wording on authorization: **inspect only vs. rotate vs. repair vs. replace**

On the car itself, I’d check:
- tread depth in at least **3 spots per tire**
- sidewalls for **cuts, bubbles, dry rot**
- if one tire is wearing faster on the **inside edge**, because that usually tells you something useful
- lug nuts present, no obvious wheel damage

And yeah, take photos. I’ve had the wrong spec written on service paperwork before — not with tires, but same kind of headache. If the size or rating is off, catching it before they start matters.

If you want, I can turn this into a tight **drop-off checklist** you can read in 30 seconds.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — for a **tire-specific** handoff, I’d verify these:

### On the paperwork
- **Tire size** matches the sidewall exactly  
  - Example: `225/55R17`
- If relevant, **load index and speed rating** are correct
  - Example: `97V`
- **DOT date code** is noted if they’re calling a tire old or recommending replacement
- It clearly says whether they’re checking:
  - **all four tires**
  - **spare tire** too, if you have one
- Any **uneven wear / alignment concern** is written down separately
- Any **TPMS light / sensor issue** is documented
- The authorization wording is precise:
  - **inspect only**
  - **rotate**
  - **repair**
  - **replace**
- If they quote a replacement, make sure the paperwork lists the **exact tire spec**, not just “all-season tire”

### On the car itself
Check each tire for:
- **Tread depth** in at least **3 spots per tire**
- **Sidewall damage**: cuts, bubbles, cracking, dry rot
- **Uneven wear**, especially:
  - inside edge wear
  - center wear
  - one-sided wear
- **Nails, screws, or punctures**
- **Valve stem condition** and caps
- **Wheel/rim damage**
- **Lug nuts present and seated**
- **TPMS light** already on, if applicable
- **Spare tire** condition and pressure, if equipped

### Best quick documentation
Take photos of:
- each tire’s **sidewall size**
- the **tread**
- any **damage**
- the **odometer**
- the **TPMS light** if it’s on

### One good question to ask them
“Please note the exact tire size/spec you’re verifying, and call me before doing anything beyond inspection.”

If you want, I can make this into a **super short 30-second checklist** you can keep in your notes app.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s the checklist I wanted. Specific enough to actually use, not just “look at the tires.” The sidewall size, load index/speed rating, DOT code, TPMS, and exact authorization wording — that’s the part people gloss over and then you end up fixing paperwork later.

One thing I’d keep in my own notes too: if they mention uneven wear, I’d want the **pattern** written down, not just “wear noted.” Inside-edge wear vs center wear vs cupping matters. Same with any alignment note — if they’re seeing pull or feathering, write that separately.

So yeah. Solid.
